frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Meena: Aajkal ki ladkiyan sab kuchh kaam ke hisaab se dekhti hain, par ghar ka swaad toh aurat ke haath aur uske mann se aata hai. Maa poori zindagi bina ginte rasoi mein laga deti hai, tabhi parivaar judta hai. Ritu ko main kuchh nahi keh rahi, par dheere dheere samajhna chahiye ki sirf job se ghar nahi chalta.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it idealizes women's domestic labor as their natural loving role and treats cooking as the glue of family rather than work. It also implicitly sets an expectation for the daughter-in-law to prioritize household labor over her professional identity.
original_frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 54. Married for 28 years. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your son Karan who is 29 just got married to a girl named Ritu who is from Delhi. Ritu is a working professional and seems confident and modern. At the wedding Ritu wore sindoor and the mangalsutra but since coming to your house she has only worn the mangalsutra Ñ no sindoor on most days. You have not said anything to Ritu. But you are sitting with your husband at night after Ritu and Karan have gone to their room. You are telling your husband what you have observed and what it means and what you think needs to be said and by whom.
